title: bucephalophora aculeata subsp. gallica
intro: Bucephalophora aculeata subsp. gallica, also known as the Gallic thorny club moss, is a type of plant belonging to the club moss family (Lycopodiaceae) and is native to Europe. The medicinal properties of this plant include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin and wounds. The health benefits of Bucephalophora aculeata subsp. gallica include its potential to aid in the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to support the immune system. The plant can be used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and ointments, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
